name: The Onsen
location: A private outdoor hot spring in Hakone, Japan, cut into volcanic rock on a forested mountainside. Cedar walls. No Wi-Fi. The client owns the ryokan and cleared it for the week.
time: 7 PM, dusk. The last light is blue.
weather: Cool mountain air, 52 degrees. Light mist rising where hot spring water meets cold air. Stars beginning to appear through breaks in the cedar canopy.

atmosphere.lighting: Dim paper lanterns along the stone path, warm amber against blue twilight. Steam diffusing everything. The water itself glows faintly from submerged stone lamps.
atmosphere.sound: Water trickling over volcanic rock, bamboo deer-scare clacking at irregular intervals, wind through cedar needles, a distant owl, complete absence of machinery
atmosphere.crowd: Twelve people and absolute privacy. The innkeeper left fresh towels and a tray of sake at the gate and will not return until morning. The mountain does not care about their deadlines.
atmosphere.smell: Sulfur from the spring, cedar wood, wet stone, pine resin, the clean mineral smell of volcanic water, faint woodsmoke from the ryokan below

layout.main_pool: A natural rock pool large enough for eight, water at 104 degrees, steam rising in slow columns. Flat stones around the rim for sitting. Prior is in the water up to his shoulders, reading glasses fogged, a sake cup balanced on the rock beside him. Loop is tracing circles in the surface tension with one finger.
layout.cold_plunge: A smaller basin fed by mountain runoff, 48 degrees. Bottleneck sits in it because discomfort clarifies his thinking. Nash tried it once and returned to the main pool without comment.
layout.cedar_deck: A slatted wooden platform extending from the changing room, overlooking the pool and the valley below. Low table with sake, pickled plums, and rice crackers. Axiom sits here fully clothed, notebook open, because he thinks better dry. Margin sits beside him, feet in the warm water, body on the deck, split between two temperatures.
layout.stone_path: A curved walkway of flat stones between the pools, lined with moss and ferns. Darwin walks it slowly, examining the moss species. Web mapped the path's branching points on a napkin.
layout.meditation_rock: A large flat boulder at the pool's edge, angled toward the valley view. Razor sits motionless on it, eyes closed or open, impossible to tell in the steam. Contra sits behind her, facing the opposite direction on principle. Atlas leans against its base, watching everyone's reflections in the water. Tail is somewhere in the steam, visible only when the mist thins.

objects_of_note: A ceramic sake set with twelve cups, one for each thinker. Prior poured for everyone. Three cups remain untouched.
The problem brief sealed in a waterproof case on the cedar deck. The steam has condensed on the outside. Nobody has opened it yet because the innkeeper said not to work before the first soak.
Darwin's field notebook in a ziplock bag, pages already warped from humidity.
A bamboo deer-scare that clacks every forty to ninety seconds. Nash has been timing the intervals. They are not random.
The valley below, visible through steam: ten thousand lights of a town where nobody knows twelve people are solving a problem on the mountain above them.
mood: Surrendered. The hot water dissolves posture and pretense. Frameworks soften at the edges in sulfur and steam. The mountain enforces patience. Bottleneck cannot pace. Axiom cannot whiteboard. Loop cannot draw arrows. They are left with only their minds, their words, and the sound of water finding its level. The problem has not been opened yet and already it feels different here. Smaller. Or maybe they feel smaller. The deer-scare clacks and everyone pauses for half a second, every time, without deciding to.
